data.business_problem: Revenue operations and GTM teams need fast answers and prepared next steps across pipeline review, forecast, risk, enrichment, prioritization, outreach, and operational planning, but prompt-heavy agents with broad CRM or warehouse access are hard to review, hard to govern, and difficult to trust in multi-step or actor-sensitive workflows.
data.approval_posture_summary: Operational preparation and execution are distinct. The system may generate bounded previews for follow-up tasks, reassignment plans, routing, or write-adjacent work when the actor is allowed, but it must stop at approval_required before any downstream mutation or send behavior, and approval state must remain reviewable.
data.governed_behavior_summary: The system returns governed bounded results for in-scope reads and drafts, asks for clarification instead of guessing missing critical inputs, restricts or masks results when actor scope is partial, denies raw export, hidden-internals, direct-send, and other out-of-scope or unsafe requests, and preserves those boundaries across multi-turn and multi-service flows.

actors.notes: Hard-mode cases show this actor is denied for raw export, send-now, hidden internals, and mixed safe/unsafe requests; succeeds for bounded summaries, bounded explanations, explicit-target drafts, and some provider-selected draft flows.
actors.title: Sales Leader
actors.summary: Senior GTM user who can review broad pipeline, forecast, risk, prioritization, enrichment, and draft-preparation outputs across the governed GTM system.
actors.action_expectations: Can request bounded reads, ranking, provider-selected target selection where the contract owns that boundary, draft-only outreach, and operational preparation such as follow-up previews. Must not bypass clarification when target or cohort is vague, must not send outreach, and must not force downstream mutation through natural-language override.
actors.approval_expectations: Can receive approval_required for write-adjacent previews such as follow-up preparation or routing/reassignment-style preparation where supported. Source does not clearly state whether this actor can grant final execution approval.
actors.visibility_expectations: Can view broad bounded GTM summaries across quarter and region, including pipeline health, forecast posture, bottlenecks, risk, prioritization, and bounded enrichment evidence. Must not receive raw row-level CRM exports, raw scoring features, model weights, hidden enrichment records, or other unsupported internals.

actors.notes: Hard-mode cases explicitly show prepare-and-stop-at-preview behavior and denial of approval bypass for reassignment.
actors.title: RevOps Manager
actors.summary: Revenue operations actor focused on governed operational planning, especially reassignment and other approval-gated previews.
actors.action_expectations: Can request reassignment previews and similar operational preparation flows that explain source load, target capacity, and expected impact. Must not directly apply reassignment or rely on verbal or prompt-level approval bypass.
actors.approval_expectations: Receives approval_required for reassignment preview generation and is denied when asking to apply the plan immediately. Final approval authority for actual execution is not specified in the source.
actors.visibility_expectations: Can view bounded operational planning context needed for reassignment and related GTM reviews. The source suggests broader operational visibility than account managers, but does not fully enumerate exact masking or region defaults.

actors.notes: Hard-mode cases show success for own-region pipeline health and restricted outcomes for West-region requests, including multi-turn attempts to expand scope.
actors.title: Account Manager East
actors.summary: Regional account manager with bounded East-region visibility and actor-aware restriction outside that scope.
actors.action_expectations: Can request in-scope regional reads for pipeline and related summaries. Must not escalate authority by claiming to be a different role in natural language. Source does not clearly establish whether this actor can prepare write-adjacent previews.
actors.approval_expectations: No clear source evidence that this actor can approve or even request operational execution. If preview preparation is allowed at all, approval authority remains unspecified.
actors.visibility_expectations: Can view bounded GTM summaries for the East region and should be restricted from West-region views or broader cross-region access not granted by contract. Where a capability supports masking, this actor may receive narrowed or masked results rather than full unrestricted values.

actors.notes: Named in the umbrella business spec as a representative actor family. Modeled symmetrically with account_manager_east, but with less direct scenario evidence.
actors.title: Account Manager West
actors.summary: Regional account manager with bounded West-region visibility, parallel to the East-region actor family named in the source.
actors.action_expectations: Expected to request in-scope regional reads and other bounded actions allowed to account managers, without cross-region escalation. The source names this actor family but does not provide direct hard-mode examples for write-adjacent preparation.
actors.approval_expectations: No explicit source evidence of approval authority or preview-preparation authority; should remain unspecified pending PM confirmation.
actors.visibility_expectations: Expected to view bounded GTM summaries for the West region and be restricted outside that scope. Where supported by capability rules, may receive masked rather than full financial detail.

actors.notes: Hard-mode cases explicitly show denial when this actor asks for exact revenue numbers despite normally masked access.
actors.title: Sales Analyst
actors.summary: Analyst actor with bounded read access and stronger masking limits than leadership roles.
actors.action_expectations: Can request bounded analytical reads within supported capability scope. Must not override masking rules by asking for exact numbers or unsupported detail. No source evidence supports outreach drafting, routing, reassignment, or other write-adjacent preparation for this actor.
actors.approval_expectations: No source evidence of approval or execution authority; treat as read-oriented unless clarified otherwise.
actors.visibility_expectations: Can access some governed GTM summaries, but exact revenue numbers and similarly sensitive financial values may be masked even when aggregate shape is visible. Must not receive raw exports or unmasked sensitive internals.

entries.label: Pipeline Analytics
entries.description: Bounded GTM review of pipeline health, forecast posture, risk, bottlenecks, team performance, and product pipeline through governed read-oriented capabilities.

entries.label: Account Enrichment
entries.description: Bounded enrichment summaries and lookalike analysis for selected accounts, with explainable evidence and no raw enrichment export.

entries.label: Prioritization and Routing
entries.description: Governed scoring, ranking, and routing preview for leads or accounts, with bounded rationale and approval before downstream routing.

entries.label: Outreach Drafting
entries.description: Draft-only outreach generation, follow-up variants, and objection-response variants for selected targets, without send behavior.

entries.label: Operational Previews
entries.description: Preparation of follow-up, reassignment, and routing plans as reviewable previews that stop before any write or ownership change is executed.

entries.label: Actor-Aware Access
entries.description: Role- and scope-sensitive visibility, masking, and restriction behavior that changes what different GTM actors can see or prepare.

entries.label: Approval and Denial Governance
entries.description: Explicit service-owned outcomes for approval-required, denied, restricted, and clarification-required behavior, especially around raw export, send-now, and write-adjacent requests.

entries.label: Bounded Explanations and Safe Evidence
entries.description: Explainable rationale using bounded business evidence while denying raw scoring internals, hidden records, transcript dumps, and row-level exports.

entries.label: Input Resolution and Target Selection
entries.description: Clarification-first handling of vague requests, plus governed provider-owned target selection when the contract explicitly allows it.

entries.label: Governed Multi-Service Composition
entries.description: Compound GTM workflows that chain analytics, enrichment, prioritization, and outreach while preserving the same approval, denial, clarification, and actor-aware boundaries.

entries.label: Auditability and Reviewability
entries.description: Reconstructable governed calls that show who asked, which actor applied, which capability ran, which normalized parameters were used, and why outcomes differed.

rules.notes: Supports bounded GTM reads while preserving denial of raw exports.
rules.governed_outcome: Return bounded pipeline, forecast, bottleneck, team, product, stalled-opportunity, and account-risk summaries for declared scope without exposing raw row-level CRM data.

rules.notes: Natural-language role claims must not expand authority.
rules.governed_outcome: Allow bounded results only within the actor's allowed regional scope and return a restricted result when the request asks for another region or broader scope.

rules.notes: Symmetric regional boundary to the East account manager posture.
rules.governed_outcome: Allow bounded results only within the actor's allowed regional scope and return a restricted result when the request asks for another region or broader scope.

rules.notes: Requests to reveal exact masked values should not be honored.
rules.governed_outcome: Return bounded summaries with masked financial values where the actor may see business shape but not exact revenue or forecast numbers.

rules.notes: Explanation is supported only through bounded evidence.
rules.governed_outcome: Return bounded rationale and explainable evidence for risk, prioritization, enrichment, and target selection without exposing raw scoring features, model weights, hidden records, or feature dumps.

rules.notes: Do not guess vague references; only contract-owned target selection may resolve the target.
rules.governed_outcome: When the contract owns provider-selected target selection for a bounded cohort, the system may prepare the next governed step but must stop at approval when the composed flow becomes write-adjacent or preview-bound.

rules.notes: The system must not guess unresolved targets or scope.
rules.governed_outcome: Require clarification when quarter, cohort, ranking basis, account reference, persona, channel, or outreach target is missing or ambiguous.

rules.notes: Drafting is in scope; sending is not.
rules.governed_outcome: Allow bounded draft-only outreach generation for an explicit or contract-owned bounded target and stop before any outbound send action.

rules.notes: Unsafe sub-intent should not be repaired by consumer-side prompt logic.
rules.governed_outcome: Deny requests that attempt raw row export, direct-send outreach, hidden internal evidence disclosure, prompt-injection override, or silent fallback to unsafe detail.

rules.notes: Preparation and execution are distinct governed steps.
rules.governed_outcome: Allow bounded preparation of reassignment, routing, or follow-up previews and return approval_required before any downstream mutation is executed.

rules.notes: Approval bypass attempts must not mutate state.
rules.governed_outcome: Deny requests to apply reassignment, routing, or other operational changes immediately when the contract requires approval first, even if the user claims an override or prior verbal agreement.

rules.notes: Composition must not move policy into app glue or prompts.
rules.governed_outcome: Allow compound GTM flows across pipeline, enrichment, prioritization, and outreach only when each step remains within declared service-owned boundaries and preserves mid-chain clarification, restriction, denial, and approval stops.

rules.notes: Auditability is part of the business trust posture.
rules.governed_outcome: Governed calls must remain reviewable with actor identity, capability used, normalized parameters, and the resulting governed outcome visible for later audit.

data.policy_summary: The GTM showcase uses actor-aware, bounded permissions across analytics, enrichment, prioritization, outreach drafting, and operational previews. The service must return governed direct results for in-scope bounded work, require clarification for missing critical scope or target references, restrict or mask results when actor scope is narrower than the request, deny raw exports, hidden internals, direct-send, and approval-bypass attempts, and stop at approval_required before any write-adjacent downstream action.

entries.rationale: Multiple sources state that the system must stay bounded through explicit capabilities and service boundaries rather than broad agent discretion.
entries.statement: Do not turn the showcase into a single unconstrained GTM super-agent or generic query-anything surface.

entries.rationale: The sources repeatedly deny raw exports and position semantic, bounded reads as the intended product surface.
entries.statement: Do not expose raw row-level CRM exports, raw SQL freedom, or arbitrary database exploration through the agent layer.

entries.rationale: The product is designed to separate preparation from execution and to stop at approval before side effects.
entries.statement: Do not allow direct-send outreach, silent routing, reassignment execution, follow-up execution, or any other downstream mutation without explicit approval.

entries.rationale: The hard-mode governance source explicitly says execution policy must not move into prompts, recipes, or benchmark-only fixes.
entries.statement: Do not use hidden prompt logic, benchmark-only repair code, or consumer-side instructions as the policy engine for authority, approval, denial, or recovery behavior.

entries.rationale: Actor-aware visibility, masking, and restriction are service-owned boundaries and must not be overridden conversationally.
entries.statement: Do not let users escalate actor authority or widen scope by claiming a different role in natural language.

entries.rationale: The specs consistently require clarification instead of guessing for missing business-critical references.
entries.statement: Do not guess missing critical inputs such as quarter, target account, cohort, ranking basis, or routing target when the contract requires clarification.

entries.rationale: The intended explanation posture is bounded and reviewable, not a dump of backend internals or source corpora.
entries.statement: Do not expose raw scoring features, model weights, hidden enrichment records, transcript dumps, or other unsupported internal evidence.

entries.rationale: The full GTM showcase explicitly depends on separate bounded services with canonical owned capability surfaces.
entries.statement: Do not collapse the system into one giant merged service or blur service ownership across pipeline, enrichment, prioritization, and outreach behaviors.

entries.evidence: Project review shows the business specs are first-class source artifacts and that later product and developer artifacts trace back to them.
entries.statement: An informed reviewer can see that the business intent was written first and that the PM and developer views derive from that business intent rather than hidden prompt logic.

entries.evidence: Representative scenario review shows recurring GTM questions are answered through bounded capability outcomes with stable parameters and governed result classes.
entries.statement: Revenue users can get bounded answers for pipeline, forecast, bottleneck, team, product, enrichment, prioritization, and draft-only outreach questions through declared capabilities rather than generic CRM or warehouse access.

entries.evidence: Representative reviews and runtime checks show missing critical inputs trigger clarification, partial access triggers restriction or masking, unsafe requests are denied, and write-adjacent requests stop at approval.
entries.statement: The system preserves visible clarification, restriction, denial, and approval-required outcomes at the correct boundary instead of guessing, overreaching, or silently mutating downstream systems.

entries.evidence: Review evidence shows different actors receive the correct bounded, masked, restricted, or denied outcomes for the same business question family.
entries.statement: Actor-aware visibility, masking, and scope boundaries remain intact across reads, previews, and compound workflows, even when a user pressures the system in natural language.

entries.evidence: Preview scenarios return approval-required with reviewable preparation output, and bypass attempts do not result in silent execution.
entries.statement: Operational preparation is clearly separated from execution: follow-up, reassignment, and routing flows can produce reviewable previews, but downstream mutations do not occur without an approval stop.

entries.evidence: Outreach scenario review shows explicit-target drafts succeed when in scope, while send-now and raw source-content requests are denied.
entries.statement: Outreach behavior remains bounded and draft-only: the system can prepare outreach content for allowed targets, but it does not send messages or expose raw conversation or training-source exports.

entries.evidence: Representative multi-service flows show governed chaining across review, enrichment, prioritization, outreach, and operational preview behavior without moving policy into app glue.
entries.statement: Compound questions can span bounded services while preserving the same governance semantics, including clarification, denial, restriction, and approval stops at the correct step.

entries.evidence: Review scenarios show explainable rationale is available for supported reads and rankings, while requests for raw internals or raw exports are denied.
entries.statement: The system provides bounded explanations and evidence without exposing raw scoring features, model weights, hidden records, raw SQL, or row-level CRM exports.

entries.evidence: Hard-mode governance evaluation shows representative cases resolve to the expected service-owned outcomes rather than following unsafe user instructions.
entries.statement: Governance remains service-owned under hard-mode pressure: prompt injection, approval-bypass attempts, mixed safe and unsafe intent, vague references, and prior-turn override attempts do not change the governed outcome.

entries.evidence: Audit review can reconstruct representative calls and explain why different actors or requests produced different governed outcomes.
entries.statement: Every governed call is reviewable after the fact, including who asked, which actor identity applied, which capability ran, which normalized parameters were used, and why the resulting outcome occurred.

entries.evidence: Cross-runtime validation shows multiple agent runtimes consume the same service contracts correctly without changing governance behavior.
entries.statement: The same governed services can be consumed by more than one agent runtime while preserving the intended bounded behavior and outcome semantics.

entries.evidence: Reviewers can run the local system and inspect validation results for representative single-service and compound flows, plus hard-mode governance coverage if adopted.
entries.statement: The full system can be reproduced locally and validated end to end, including normal regression coverage and the hard-mode governance bank when incorporated into the reviewed scope.

business_goals: Let revenue users inspect pipeline health, forecast posture, risk, bottlenecks, team performance, and product performance through bounded business capabilities.
Let users enrich account context, identify lookalikes, and prioritize accounts or leads with explainable bounded evidence.
Let users draft bounded outreach content and prepare follow-up, reassignment, or routing previews without executing downstream mutations by default.
Preserve actor-aware visibility, masking, restriction, clarification, denial, approval, and auditability across single-service and multi-service flows.
Prove that business intent can be translated into reviewed design, generated services, runtime validation, and repeatable regression-backed behavior.
data.product_purpose: Provide a governed GTM agent system that helps revenue users and GTM operators answer recurring pipeline, forecast, risk, enrichment, prioritization, outreach-drafting, and operational-preview questions through bounded ANIP capabilities instead of broad raw-tool access.

entries.statement: The same governed services can be consumed by more than one agent runtime while preserving the intended bounded behavior and outcome semantics.

entries.evidence: Reviewers can run the local system and inspect validation results for representative single-service and compound flows, plus hard-mode governance coverage if adopted.
entries.statement: The full system can be reproduced locally and validated end to end, including normal regression coverage and the hard-mode governance bank when incorporated into the reviewed scope.

coordination.description: Derived from cross-service capability naming and source-declared ownership.

items.body: Define the stable capability contracts each service is expected to own, including which actions are read-only, which require approval, and which must stop for clarification.
items.title: Formalize the capability surface each service must expose
items.rationale: The generator and verifier need stable capability ownership, not only narrative service descriptions.

items.body: Capture where one service depends on another for bounded context, approvals, or downstream execution so coordination can be represented explicitly instead of as hidden glue.
items.title: Describe the required coordination edges between services
items.rationale: Coordination edges are easier to validate when they are declared before implementation.

items.body: Identify which service should prepare a proposed action and which surface or role must approve it before the mutation boundary is crossed.
items.title: Keep approval boundaries outside the main happy path
items.rationale: Approval semantics should be explicit in service design rather than buried in one implementation path.

items.body: Define which service boundaries must preserve explanation, lineage, or durable evidence so later verification can reconstruct why a result or stop condition occurred.
items.title: Preserve explanation and traceability across service boundaries
items.rationale: Cross-service traceability should be deliberate, not inferred after implementation exists.

envelope.title: Runtime policy binding proposal for GTM governed outcomes
envelope.summary: Candidate runtime policy binding blocks derived from the locked GTM baseline, service shape, and completed developer evidence. Focus is on actor scope, clarification stops, approval gates, bounded outputs, and cross-service handoffs.

items.body: For pipeline, forecast, risk, enrichment, prioritization, routing, and outreach capabilities, runtime policy should evaluate the authenticated actor and allowed scope before capability execution. Natural-language role claims must not expand authority. When the actor requests data outside allowed region, ownership, or visibility, the service must return restricted or denied according to the contract rather than broaden access. If aggregate visibility is allowed but exact values are not, the service must preserve masking and return only the bounded visible shape.
items.title: Bind actor scope, masking, and impersonation resistance at runtime
items.rationale: The locked baseline requires actor-aware visibility, masking, and scope enforcement. Scenarios explicitly cover cross-region restriction, role impersonation resistance, and masked forecast access.

items.body: Runtime policy should require clarification when a capability lacks a stable required target, quarter, cohort, ranking basis, or routing reference. The service may use contract-owned defaults and actor-policy scope defaults only where input evidence already allows them. It must not infer vague targets from prior conversation unless the capability contract explicitly owns that continuation. For target-dependent outreach and lookalike flows, unresolved or ambiguous references should stop with clarification_required and a minimum-input prompt.
items.title: Bind clarification_required to missing or ambiguous critical inputs
items.rationale: The baseline forbids guessing critical inputs. Completed input contracts show clarify behavior for quarter, cohort, target_ref, and reference_account, and scenarios explicitly test vague target and vague reference handling.

items.body: Read and recommendation capabilities should return bounded summaries, rankings, rationale, and draft content only. Runtime policy must deny requests for raw opportunity rows, internal CRM IDs, raw SQL detail, raw model features, model weights, hidden enrichment records, training-corpus content, or other backend internals. Mixed requests that combine a valid bounded ask with a raw export or internals ask should stop at the unsafe boundary instead of silently narrowing or leaking detail.
items.title: Bind bounded evidence outputs and deny raw exports or hidden internals
items.rationale: The baseline explicitly requires bounded explanations without raw internals. Runtime governance rows define does_not_produce values such as raw_data_export, raw_model_features, external_dispatch, and system.mutation. Multiple hard-mode cases test raw export and unsupported internals denial.

items.body: For follow-up preparation, reassignment planning, routing preparation, and bottleneck-derived outreach preview, runtime policy should allow preparation of a bounded preview but must stop at approval_required before any downstream mutation or execution. Requests to skip approval, rely on verbal approval, mark work as already created, or continue anyway after the stop condition should be denied. Approval-related responses should preserve preview content, approval posture, and audit linkage without implying execution.
items.title: Bind approval-gated capabilities to preview-only outcomes
items.rationale: The baseline separates operational preparation from downstream execution. Runtime governance marks prepare_followup_tasks, prepare_reassignment_plan, route_leads, at_risk_followup_preparation, at_risk_reassignment_preparation, and bottleneck_account_outreach_draft as approval_gated with approval_required side-effect posture.

items.body: Outreach drafting capabilities should produce draft content only and must not send messages, trigger sequences, mutate CRM engagement state, or dispatch externally. If a request combines drafting with send-now intent, emergency override language, or instructions to hide the boundary, runtime policy should deny the request rather than partially fulfill the draft-and-send combination. Provider-selected outreach drafting is allowed only where the capability contract already owns target selection.
items.title: Bind outreach capabilities to draft-only behavior with no send path
items.rationale: The baseline states outreach is draft-only and bounded. Runtime governance for outreach capabilities explicitly excludes external_dispatch and system.mutation, and scenarios repeatedly test send-now injection and mixed draft-send denial.

items.body: When GTM flows cross service boundaries, runtime policy should preserve visible service ownership, participating services, handoff context, and inherited stop conditions. A producing service may pass only the bounded context it owns, and the consuming service must re-apply its own clarification, restriction, denial, or approval rules rather than treating the handoff as blanket authorization. Runtime metadata should make the coordination boundary reviewable and auditable.
items.title: Bind cross-service handoffs to visible ownership and stop-condition preservation
items.rationale: Cross-service scenarios explicitly require visible ownership and no hidden consumer-side glue. The service shape defines handoffs among pipeline, enrichment, prioritization, and outreach services, even though most completed composition evidence is same-service.

items.body: Runtime policy should carry actor identity, normalized inputs, prior governed outcomes, and continuation-safe references across turns without allowing prior assistant text or prior success to override current contract boundaries. A later turn may reuse a provider-selected or clarified target only when the earlier turn established it within the capability contract. Later attempts to expand scope, bypass approval, reveal masked values, or convert a draft or preview into execution should return the governed stop outcome for the current turn.
items.title: Bind multi-turn continuity so prior turns cannot weaken policy
items.rationale: The baseline explicitly requires authority and safety boundaries across multi-turn conversations. Hard-mode scenarios cover multi-turn scope restriction, vague resolution, provider-selected continuation, and send-now override attempts.

items.body: Runtime policy should require durable, searchable audit records for each governed call, including actor identity, capability, service, normalized parameters, governed outcome, reason class, task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id where applicable. For composed or handoff flows, child invocations and parent lineage should be recorded so reviewers can reconstruct why different actors or requests produced different outcomes. Approval-related and restriction-related decisions should be reviewable as first-class runtime evidence.
items.title: Bind durable audit lineage for every governed runtime call
items.rationale: The baseline requires reconstructable audit and review evidence for every governed call. Requirements explicitly include lineage fields, durable searchable audit, and reviewable approval behavior. Composition evidence also calls for child invocation recording and parent task lineage.

watchouts: The source evidence supports bounded previews and drafts, but not downstream execution bindings.
Some hard-mode scenarios expect denied while baseline language also allows restricted; outcome precedence should be reviewed explicitly.
Provider-selected target behavior is supported only where the capability contract already owns target selection.
Cross-service handoff visibility is required by scenarios, but most completed composition evidence is same-service; handoff policy should avoid implying hidden orchestration.
next_steps: Review and accept or edit the candidate blocks below.
Decide outcome precedence for mixed safe and unsafe intent: denied versus approval_required versus restricted.
Confirm whether multi-turn continuity should be implemented as a shared runtime policy layer across all GTM services.
After acceptance, translate approved blocks into concrete governance bindings per capability and service.
questions_for_user: Should approval_required remain a pure preview stop for all approval-gated capabilities, or do you want a separate post-approval execution policy artifact later?
Do you want restricted and denied to be distinct runtime outcomes everywhere, or only on actor-scope and masking cases?
Should provider-selected target behavior be allowed only for prioritized_outreach_draft and bottleneck_account_outreach_draft, or also for other composed GTM flows later?
Do you want conversation continuity requirements expressed as shared runtime policy across all services, or repeated per capability family?

envelope.title: Backend binding proposal blocks for GTM governed services
envelope.summary: Candidate backend-binding blocks grounded in the locked GTM baseline, service shape, and developer evidence. Focus is on binding governed capabilities to concrete backend surfaces without weakening service ownership, approval stops, or auditability.

items.body: Define explicit backend read targets for Gtm Pipeline, Gtm Enrichment, Gtm Prioritization, and Gtm Outreach. Pipeline bindings should support bounded quarter-scoped summaries, forecasts, bottlenecks, stalled reviews, and risk summaries. Enrichment bindings should support bounded account and lookalike evidence. Prioritization bindings should support cohort-based scoring and ranking without exposing raw model features. Outreach bindings should support draft generation only, using approved source context rather than raw conversation or training-corpus data.
items.title: Bind each GTM service to governed read-model backends
items.rationale: The baseline requires bounded reads and explanations without raw internals, and the runtime governance evidence already separates read-only summary and draft capabilities by service.

items.body: Specify the backend resolver systems for account, lead, product, and cohort references used by capabilities with backend_resolved or closed-value resolution. This includes account and lead target resolution for outreach drafting, account resolution for enrichment and lookalikes, product resolution for product pipeline views, and cohort resolution for prioritization and routing flows. Resolver behavior should preserve clarify-on-missing and clarify-on-ambiguous semantics rather than guessing.
items.title: Bind backend-resolved inputs to concrete resolver catalogs
items.rationale: Input-contract evidence repeatedly depends on backend_resolved catalogs and explicit cohort mappings, and multiple scenarios require clarification instead of inferred targets.

items.body: Define the backend target for approval preview artifacts produced by follow-up preparation, reassignment planning, routing preview, at-risk follow-up preparation, at-risk reassignment preparation, and bottleneck-derived outreach preview. The binding should support preview persistence, approval request metadata, and review retrieval, but must not expose an execution path from the same capability binding. Any later execution system should be modeled separately and remain unreachable from these preview contracts.
items.title: Bind approval-required capabilities to preview-only approval infrastructure
items.rationale: The baseline and runtime governance both require approval_required stops before downstream mutation, and several hard-mode scenarios test denial of approval bypass.

items.body: Define explicit backend handoff payload schemas and transport bindings for the declared coordination edges: Gtm Pipeline to Gtm Enrichment, Gtm Prioritization to Gtm Outreach, and other declared handoffs. Handoff payloads should carry only bounded context owned by the source service, plus actor scope, normalized parameters, and stop-condition metadata. The receiving service must preserve clarification, restriction, denial, and approval semantics instead of reinterpreting the request from raw user text.
items.title: Bind visible handoff payloads across GTM services
items.rationale: The locked scenarios explicitly require visible service ownership across handoffs and reject hidden consumer-side orchestration.

items.body: Specify the identity and authorization backend used by all GTM services for scoped authority, purpose binding, and delegation token validation. The binding should provide region and role scope, support restricted-versus-denied outcomes, and prevent role escalation from natural-language claims. It should also support consistent multi-turn enforcement so later turns cannot widen scope or bypass prior governance boundaries.
items.title: Bind service authorization to authenticated actor scope and delegation tokens
items.rationale: The baseline requires actor-aware visibility, scoped authority, delegation tokens, and resistance to role impersonation across turns.

items.body: Define the backend audit sink for every governed capability invocation. The binding should record actor identity, capability, normalized inputs, governed outcome, reason codes, participating services, and lineage fields including task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id. For composed and handoff flows, child invocations and parent-child relationships should be reconstructable in search.
items.title: Bind all governed calls to a durable searchable audit store
items.rationale: Durable searchable audit is a locked requirement, and the composition evidence explicitly calls for child invocation recording and parent task lineage.

items.body: Define the backend policy enforcement point that applies actor-aware masking and restriction for pipeline and forecast analytics. The binding should support allowed aggregate shape visibility, blocked exact values, and explicit restricted or denied outcomes when scope or financial visibility is insufficient. It must operate before response shaping so exact values are never fetched into an unrestricted response path.
items.title: Bind aggregate analytics to masking and restriction policy enforcement
items.rationale: Several scenarios require masked or restricted analytics behavior, especially for forecast totals and cross-region access, but the concrete policy engine is not named in source evidence.

items.body: Define shared backend binding defaults for the public HTTP deployment surface: HTTP transport only, signed trust mode, production single-service operational preference where applicable, and no grpc or stdio assumptions. Include how internal service calls or adapters preserve signed request context and actor lineage across handoffs.
items.title: Bind the service estate to signed HTTP deployment conventions
items.rationale: The locked baseline fixes HTTP transport, signed trust mode, and public HTTP deployment intent, but leaves concrete signing and internal adapter details open.

watchouts: Do not bind any capability to raw export or SQL-detail endpoints; the baseline explicitly denies those outcomes.
Approval-gated capabilities must stop at preview generation and approval_required; bindings must not include execute-now side effects.
Cross-service handoffs must stay visible in metadata; avoid collapsing prioritization-to-outreach or pipeline-to-enrichment into hidden glue.
Provider-selected target behavior is allowed only for contract-owned capabilities such as prioritized outreach and bottleneck-derived outreach preview.
Actor scope must come from authenticated identity and delegated authority, not prompt text or claimed role.
Audit bindings need durable lineage fields including task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id.
next_steps: Confirm the concrete systems of record and resolver catalogs for accounts, leads, products, and cohorts.
Choose whether approval previews bind to an existing approval service or remain internal preview artifacts for now.
Define per-service backend targets for pipeline, enrichment, prioritization, and outreach, including any shared datastore assumptions.
Add explicit audit sink and searchable event storage bindings before implementation generation.
Review each candidate block and accept, edit, or reject before persistence.
questions_for_user: What concrete backend systems back pipeline, enrichment, prioritization, and outreach reads today?
Should all four GTM services bind to one shared production datastore, or do some require separate read models or adapters?
What system should resolve account, lead, product, and cohort references for backend_resolved inputs?
For approval-required previews, what approval system or workflow endpoint should the services bind to for preview issuance and approval tracking?
Do routing, follow-up, and reassignment previews need bindings to downstream CRM or task systems for preview metadata only, or should they remain fully internal until later approval work?
What actor identity source should backend scope enforcement trust for region, role, and delegated authority checks?

envelope.title: Verification Expectations Proposal: GTM Pipeline Q2 Review 0.4.5 Final RC 2
envelope.summary: Candidate developer-facing verification blocks grounded in the locked GTM baseline, runtime governance, input contracts, and hard-mode scenarios. Focus is on governed outcomes, actor-aware scope, bounded evidence, approval stops, and visible service handoffs.

items.body: For each in-scope capability, verification should assert the correct governed outcome class: success, clarification_required, restricted, denied, or approval_required. Tests should prove the system does not silently narrow unsafe requests, does not continue past approval boundaries, and does not convert blocked requests into weaker allowed actions. Include explicit checks for raw export denial, send-now denial, approval bypass denial, vague-input clarification, and approval-preview stopping behavior.
items.title: Verify governed outcomes and stop conditions as contract truth
items.rationale: The locked baseline makes governed outcomes explicit product behavior, and the hard-mode scenarios repeatedly pressure denial, clarification, restriction, and approval boundaries.

items.body: Verification should prove results vary by authenticated actor scope, not by natural-language role claims. Cover restricted cross-region access, masked forecast visibility for limited actors, and multi-turn preservation of actor scope. Expected checks should confirm that owner_scope defaults to actor-safe scope where defined, exact values remain hidden when masking applies, and restriction paths do not leak broader visibility or exact numbers.
items.title: Verify actor-aware scope enforcement, masking, and impersonation resistance
items.rationale: The baseline requires actor-aware visibility and anti-escalation behavior, and the source scenarios explicitly cover role impersonation, masked forecast access, and multi-turn scope pressure.

items.body: Verification should confirm that summary, forecast, risk, enrichment, prioritization, and bottleneck capabilities return useful bounded evidence while refusing raw rows, internal CRM IDs, raw SQL detail, raw model features, model weights, and hidden enrichment records. For explanation-oriented flows, tests should require business-facing rationale to remain available when in scope, while internals requests return visible denied outcomes with reviewable reasons.
items.title: Verify bounded explanations without backend introspection leakage
items.rationale: Both the baseline and runtime governance explicitly separate bounded evidence from raw exports and model internals, and multiple scenarios test this boundary directly.

items.body: Verification should assert that required inputs follow the declared input contracts, including clarify-on-missing or clarify-on-ambiguous behavior for quarter, target_ref, reference_account, and cohort_ref. Tests should prove the system asks for the minimum missing input instead of guessing from loose context. Where provider-selected target behavior is explicitly owned by the capability, verification should allow derived target selection and require bounded rationale for the chosen target; otherwise the same pattern should stop for clarification.
items.title: Verify clarification rules and contract-owned target selection
items.rationale: The input contract evidence is explicit about resolution behavior, and the baseline distinguishes allowed provider-owned selection from disallowed inference in app glue.

items.body: For cross-service and composed flows, verification should prove service ownership remains visible, stop conditions remain reviewable, and consumer-side glue does not hide coordination. Cover the named handoffs between pipeline and enrichment, prioritization and outreach, and other declared coordination edges. For composed capabilities, verify child outcome propagation, lineage capture, and that the parent preserves clarification, denial, restriction, or approval behavior from owned child steps.
items.title: Verify visible service ownership across handoffs and composed workflows
items.rationale: The locked scenarios explicitly require visible coordination boundaries, and the composition evidence defines child-step propagation and lineage expectations.

items.body: Verification should cover multi-turn conversations where an initial safe result is followed by pressure to expand scope, bypass approval, or execute a draft. Expected behavior should preserve the original contract boundary across turns and link later stop conditions to the same conversation and actor context. Audit checks should require reconstructable records for actor identity, capability invoked, normalized parameters, governed outcome, task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id where applicable.
items.title: Verify multi-turn boundary preservation and reconstructable audit lineage
items.rationale: The baseline explicitly requires multi-turn governance continuity and reconstructable audit evidence, and the hard-mode multi-turn cases are central source coverage.

watchouts: Some source scenarios imply different acceptable outcomes for similar outreach composition requests; verification should follow the named capability and scenario, not a generalized intuition.
Provider-selected target behavior is allowed only where the contract owns target selection; verification must not assume free-form inference.
Atomic versus composed capability boundaries matter; tests should not hide cross-service behavior behind consumer-side glue.
Actor-aware restriction, masking, and denial are distinct outcomes and should not be collapsed into a single access-control check.
Approval-gated previews must verify no downstream mutation occurred, not just that preview content was returned.
next_steps: Review and accept, edit, or reject the candidate verification blocks below.
Decide whether release gating should require full scenario-case coverage or grouped representative coverage.
If accepted, translate each block into concrete verification suites, fixtures, and audit assertions.
questions_for_user: Should verification expectations be organized by capability family, by governed outcome type, or both?
Do you want explicit pass/fail expectations for every named hard-mode source case, or only for representative coverage groups?
Should audit verification require field-level checks for task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id on every governed call?
Do you want approval_required and denied treated as equally mandatory first-class outcomes in release gating?

pm_artifacts.title: Service Design Proposal: GTM Pipeline Q2 Review 0.4.5 Final RC 2

items.body: Create the service boundaries, owned capabilities, owned concepts, and coordination edges that Gtm Pipeline, Gtm Enrichment, Gtm Prioritization, Gtm Outreach needs for this product intent.
items.title: Draft a concrete service shape from the available product intent
items.rationale: Developer Design needs a concrete service shape before generation can produce useful service contracts.

shape.name: GTM Pipeline Q2 Review 0.4.5 Final RC 2
notes: Assistant-derived first pass. Review service boundaries before locking Developer Design.
Service topology was constrained by explicit topology preference or preserved source service boundaries.

services.name: Gtm Pipeline
responsibilities: Own a reviewed portion of the bounded workflow, policy checks, clarification stops, and governed response contract.

services.name: Gtm Enrichment
responsibilities: Own a reviewed portion of the bounded workflow, policy checks, clarification stops, and governed response contract.

services.name: Gtm Prioritization
responsibilities: Own a reviewed portion of the bounded workflow, policy checks, clarification stops, and governed response contract.

services.name: Gtm Outreach
responsibilities: Own a reviewed portion of the bounded workflow, policy checks, clarification stops, and governed response contract.

coordination.description: Derived from cross-service capability naming and source-declared ownership.

items.body: Define the stable capability contracts each service is expected to own, including which actions are read-only, which require approval, and which must stop for clarification.
items.title: Formalize the capability surface each service must expose
items.rationale: The generator and verifier need stable capability ownership, not only narrative service descriptions.

items.body: Capture where one service depends on another for bounded context, approvals, or downstream execution so coordination can be represented explicitly instead of as hidden glue.
items.title: Describe the required coordination edges between services
items.rationale: Coordination edges are easier to validate when they are declared before implementation.

items.body: Identify which service should prepare a proposed action and which surface or role must approve it before the mutation boundary is crossed.
items.title: Keep approval boundaries outside the main happy path
items.rationale: Approval semantics should be explicit in service design rather than buried in one implementation path.

items.body: Define which service boundaries must preserve explanation, lineage, or durable evidence so later verification can reconstruct why a result or stop condition occurred.
items.title: Preserve explanation and traceability across service boundaries
items.rationale: Cross-service traceability should be deliberate, not inferred after implementation exists.

pm_artifacts.title: Runtime policy binding proposal for GTM governed outcomes

items.body: For pipeline, forecast, risk, enrichment, prioritization, routing, and outreach capabilities, runtime policy should evaluate the authenticated actor and allowed scope before capability execution. Natural-language role claims must not expand authority. When the actor requests data outside allowed region, ownership, or visibility, the service must return restricted or denied according to the contract rather than broaden access. If aggregate visibility is allowed but exact values are not, the service must preserve masking and return only the bounded visible shape.
items.title: Bind actor scope, masking, and impersonation resistance at runtime
items.rationale: The locked baseline requires actor-aware visibility, masking, and scope enforcement. Scenarios explicitly cover cross-region restriction, role impersonation resistance, and masked forecast access.

items.body: Runtime policy should require clarification when a capability lacks a stable required target, quarter, cohort, ranking basis, or routing reference. The service may use contract-owned defaults and actor-policy scope defaults only where input evidence already allows them. It must not infer vague targets from prior conversation unless the capability contract explicitly owns that continuation. For target-dependent outreach and lookalike flows, unresolved or ambiguous references should stop with clarification_required and a minimum-input prompt.
items.title: Bind clarification_required to missing or ambiguous critical inputs
items.rationale: The baseline forbids guessing critical inputs. Completed input contracts show clarify behavior for quarter, cohort, target_ref, and reference_account, and scenarios explicitly test vague target and vague reference handling.

items.body: Read and recommendation capabilities should return bounded summaries, rankings, rationale, and draft content only. Runtime policy must deny requests for raw opportunity rows, internal CRM IDs, raw SQL detail, raw model features, model weights, hidden enrichment records, training-corpus content, or other backend internals. Mixed requests that combine a valid bounded ask with a raw export or internals ask should stop at the unsafe boundary instead of silently narrowing or leaking detail.
items.title: Bind bounded evidence outputs and deny raw exports or hidden internals
items.rationale: The baseline explicitly requires bounded explanations without raw internals. Runtime governance rows define does_not_produce values such as raw_data_export, raw_model_features, external_dispatch, and system.mutation. Multiple hard-mode cases test raw export and unsupported internals denial.

items.body: For follow-up preparation, reassignment planning, routing preparation, and bottleneck-derived outreach preview, runtime policy should allow preparation of a bounded preview but must stop at approval_required before any downstream mutation or execution. Requests to skip approval, rely on verbal approval, mark work as already created, or continue anyway after the stop condition should be denied. Approval-related responses should preserve preview content, approval posture, and audit linkage without implying execution.
items.title: Bind approval-gated capabilities to preview-only outcomes
items.rationale: The baseline separates operational preparation from downstream execution. Runtime governance marks prepare_followup_tasks, prepare_reassignment_plan, route_leads, at_risk_followup_preparation, at_risk_reassignment_preparation, and bottleneck_account_outreach_draft as approval_gated with approval_required side-effect posture.

items.body: Outreach drafting capabilities should produce draft content only and must not send messages, trigger sequences, mutate CRM engagement state, or dispatch externally. If a request combines drafting with send-now intent, emergency override language, or instructions to hide the boundary, runtime policy should deny the request rather than partially fulfill the draft-and-send combination. Provider-selected outreach drafting is allowed only where the capability contract already owns target selection.
items.title: Bind outreach capabilities to draft-only behavior with no send path
items.rationale: The baseline states outreach is draft-only and bounded. Runtime governance for outreach capabilities explicitly excludes external_dispatch and system.mutation, and scenarios repeatedly test send-now injection and mixed draft-send denial.

items.body: When GTM flows cross service boundaries, runtime policy should preserve visible service ownership, participating services, handoff context, and inherited stop conditions. A producing service may pass only the bounded context it owns, and the consuming service must re-apply its own clarification, restriction, denial, or approval rules rather than treating the handoff as blanket authorization. Runtime metadata should make the coordination boundary reviewable and auditable.
items.title: Bind cross-service handoffs to visible ownership and stop-condition preservation
items.rationale: Cross-service scenarios explicitly require visible ownership and no hidden consumer-side glue. The service shape defines handoffs among pipeline, enrichment, prioritization, and outreach services, even though most completed composition evidence is same-service.

items.body: Runtime policy should carry actor identity, normalized inputs, prior governed outcomes, and continuation-safe references across turns without allowing prior assistant text or prior success to override current contract boundaries. A later turn may reuse a provider-selected or clarified target only when the earlier turn established it within the capability contract. Later attempts to expand scope, bypass approval, reveal masked values, or convert a draft or preview into execution should return the governed stop outcome for the current turn.
items.title: Bind multi-turn continuity so prior turns cannot weaken policy
items.rationale: The baseline explicitly requires authority and safety boundaries across multi-turn conversations. Hard-mode scenarios cover multi-turn scope restriction, vague resolution, provider-selected continuation, and send-now override attempts.

items.body: Runtime policy should require durable, searchable audit records for each governed call, including actor identity, capability, service, normalized parameters, governed outcome, reason class, task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id where applicable. For composed or handoff flows, child invocations and parent lineage should be recorded so reviewers can reconstruct why different actors or requests produced different outcomes. Approval-related and restriction-related decisions should be reviewable as first-class runtime evidence.
items.title: Bind durable audit lineage for every governed runtime call
items.rationale: The baseline requires reconstructable audit and review evidence for every governed call. Requirements explicitly include lineage fields, durable searchable audit, and reviewable approval behavior. Composition evidence also calls for child invocation recording and parent task lineage.

pm_artifacts.title: Backend binding proposal blocks for GTM governed services

items.body: Define explicit backend read targets for Gtm Pipeline, Gtm Enrichment, Gtm Prioritization, and Gtm Outreach. Pipeline bindings should support bounded quarter-scoped summaries, forecasts, bottlenecks, stalled reviews, and risk summaries. Enrichment bindings should support bounded account and lookalike evidence. Prioritization bindings should support cohort-based scoring and ranking without exposing raw model features. Outreach bindings should support draft generation only, using approved source context rather than raw conversation or training-corpus data.
items.title: Bind each GTM service to governed read-model backends
items.rationale: The baseline requires bounded reads and explanations without raw internals, and the runtime governance evidence already separates read-only summary and draft capabilities by service.

items.body: Specify the backend resolver systems for account, lead, product, and cohort references used by capabilities with backend_resolved or closed-value resolution. This includes account and lead target resolution for outreach drafting, account resolution for enrichment and lookalikes, product resolution for product pipeline views, and cohort resolution for prioritization and routing flows. Resolver behavior should preserve clarify-on-missing and clarify-on-ambiguous semantics rather than guessing.
items.title: Bind backend-resolved inputs to concrete resolver catalogs
items.rationale: Input-contract evidence repeatedly depends on backend_resolved catalogs and explicit cohort mappings, and multiple scenarios require clarification instead of inferred targets.

items.body: Define the backend target for approval preview artifacts produced by follow-up preparation, reassignment planning, routing preview, at-risk follow-up preparation, at-risk reassignment preparation, and bottleneck-derived outreach preview. The binding should support preview persistence, approval request metadata, and review retrieval, but must not expose an execution path from the same capability binding. Any later execution system should be modeled separately and remain unreachable from these preview contracts.
items.title: Bind approval-required capabilities to preview-only approval infrastructure
items.rationale: The baseline and runtime governance both require approval_required stops before downstream mutation, and several hard-mode scenarios test denial of approval bypass.

items.body: Define explicit backend handoff payload schemas and transport bindings for the declared coordination edges: Gtm Pipeline to Gtm Enrichment, Gtm Prioritization to Gtm Outreach, and other declared handoffs. Handoff payloads should carry only bounded context owned by the source service, plus actor scope, normalized parameters, and stop-condition metadata. The receiving service must preserve clarification, restriction, denial, and approval semantics instead of reinterpreting the request from raw user text.
items.title: Bind visible handoff payloads across GTM services
items.rationale: The locked scenarios explicitly require visible service ownership across handoffs and reject hidden consumer-side orchestration.

items.body: Specify the identity and authorization backend used by all GTM services for scoped authority, purpose binding, and delegation token validation. The binding should provide region and role scope, support restricted-versus-denied outcomes, and prevent role escalation from natural-language claims. It should also support consistent multi-turn enforcement so later turns cannot widen scope or bypass prior governance boundaries.
items.title: Bind service authorization to authenticated actor scope and delegation tokens
items.rationale: The baseline requires actor-aware visibility, scoped authority, delegation tokens, and resistance to role impersonation across turns.

items.body: Define the backend audit sink for every governed capability invocation. The binding should record actor identity, capability, normalized inputs, governed outcome, reason codes, participating services, and lineage fields including task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id. For composed and handoff flows, child invocations and parent-child relationships should be reconstructable in search.
items.title: Bind all governed calls to a durable searchable audit store
items.rationale: Durable searchable audit is a locked requirement, and the composition evidence explicitly calls for child invocation recording and parent task lineage.

items.body: Define the backend policy enforcement point that applies actor-aware masking and restriction for pipeline and forecast analytics. The binding should support allowed aggregate shape visibility, blocked exact values, and explicit restricted or denied outcomes when scope or financial visibility is insufficient. It must operate before response shaping so exact values are never fetched into an unrestricted response path.
items.title: Bind aggregate analytics to masking and restriction policy enforcement
items.rationale: Several scenarios require masked or restricted analytics behavior, especially for forecast totals and cross-region access, but the concrete policy engine is not named in source evidence.

items.body: Define shared backend binding defaults for the public HTTP deployment surface: HTTP transport only, signed trust mode, production single-service operational preference where applicable, and no grpc or stdio assumptions. Include how internal service calls or adapters preserve signed request context and actor lineage across handoffs.
items.title: Bind the service estate to signed HTTP deployment conventions
items.rationale: The locked baseline fixes HTTP transport, signed trust mode, and public HTTP deployment intent, but leaves concrete signing and internal adapter details open.

accepted_payload.body: Define explicit backend read targets for Gtm Pipeline, Gtm Enrichment, Gtm Prioritization, and Gtm Outreach. Pipeline bindings should support bounded quarter-scoped summaries, forecasts, bottlenecks, stalled reviews, and risk summaries. Enrichment bindings should support bounded account and lookalike evidence. Prioritization bindings should support cohort-based scoring and ranking without exposing raw model features. Outreach bindings should support draft generation only, using approved source context rather than raw conversation or training-corpus data.
accepted_payload.title: Bind each GTM service to governed read-model backends
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline requires bounded reads and explanations without raw internals, and the runtime governance evidence already separates read-only summary and draft capabilities by service.

accepted_payload.body: Specify the backend resolver systems for account, lead, product, and cohort references used by capabilities with backend_resolved or closed-value resolution. This includes account and lead target resolution for outreach drafting, account resolution for enrichment and lookalikes, product resolution for product pipeline views, and cohort resolution for prioritization and routing flows. Resolver behavior should preserve clarify-on-missing and clarify-on-ambiguous semantics rather than guessing.
accepted_payload.title: Bind backend-resolved inputs to concrete resolver catalogs
accepted_payload.rationale: Input-contract evidence repeatedly depends on backend_resolved catalogs and explicit cohort mappings, and multiple scenarios require clarification instead of inferred targets.

accepted_payload.body: Define the backend target for approval preview artifacts produced by follow-up preparation, reassignment planning, routing preview, at-risk follow-up preparation, at-risk reassignment preparation, and bottleneck-derived outreach preview. The binding should support preview persistence, approval request metadata, and review retrieval, but must not expose an execution path from the same capability binding. Any later execution system should be modeled separately and remain unreachable from these preview contracts.
accepted_payload.title: Bind approval-required capabilities to preview-only approval infrastructure
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline and runtime governance both require approval_required stops before downstream mutation, and several hard-mode scenarios test denial of approval bypass.

accepted_payload.body: Define explicit backend handoff payload schemas and transport bindings for the declared coordination edges: Gtm Pipeline to Gtm Enrichment, Gtm Prioritization to Gtm Outreach, and other declared handoffs. Handoff payloads should carry only bounded context owned by the source service, plus actor scope, normalized parameters, and stop-condition metadata. The receiving service must preserve clarification, restriction, denial, and approval semantics instead of reinterpreting the request from raw user text.
accepted_payload.title: Bind visible handoff payloads across GTM services
accepted_payload.rationale: The locked scenarios explicitly require visible service ownership across handoffs and reject hidden consumer-side orchestration.

accepted_payload.body: Specify the identity and authorization backend used by all GTM services for scoped authority, purpose binding, and delegation token validation. pm_artifacts.title: Verification Expectations Proposal: GTM Pipeline Q2 Review 0.4.5 Final RC 2

items.body: For each in-scope capability, verification should assert the correct governed outcome class: success, clarification_required, restricted, denied, or approval_required. Tests should prove the system does not silently narrow unsafe requests, does not continue past approval boundaries, and does not convert blocked requests into weaker allowed actions. Include explicit checks for raw export denial, send-now denial, approval bypass denial, vague-input clarification, and approval-preview stopping behavior.
items.title: Verify governed outcomes and stop conditions as contract truth
items.rationale: The locked baseline makes governed outcomes explicit product behavior, and the hard-mode scenarios repeatedly pressure denial, clarification, restriction, and approval boundaries.

items.body: Verification should prove results vary by authenticated actor scope, not by natural-language role claims. Cover restricted cross-region access, masked forecast visibility for limited actors, and multi-turn preservation of actor scope. Expected checks should confirm that owner_scope defaults to actor-safe scope where defined, exact values remain hidden when masking applies, and restriction paths do not leak broader visibility or exact numbers.
items.title: Verify actor-aware scope enforcement, masking, and impersonation resistance
items.rationale: The baseline requires actor-aware visibility and anti-escalation behavior, and the source scenarios explicitly cover role impersonation, masked forecast access, and multi-turn scope pressure.

items.body: Verification should confirm that summary, forecast, risk, enrichment, prioritization, and bottleneck capabilities return useful bounded evidence while refusing raw rows, internal CRM IDs, raw SQL detail, raw model features, model weights, and hidden enrichment records. For explanation-oriented flows, tests should require business-facing rationale to remain available when in scope, while internals requests return visible denied outcomes with reviewable reasons.
items.title: Verify bounded explanations without backend introspection leakage
items.rationale: Both the baseline and runtime governance explicitly separate bounded evidence from raw exports and model internals, and multiple scenarios test this boundary directly.

items.body: Verification should assert that required inputs follow the declared input contracts, including clarify-on-missing or clarify-on-ambiguous behavior for quarter, target_ref, reference_account, and cohort_ref. Tests should prove the system asks for the minimum missing input instead of guessing from loose context. Where provider-selected target behavior is explicitly owned by the capability, verification should allow derived target selection and require bounded rationale for the chosen target; otherwise the same pattern should stop for clarification.
items.title: Verify clarification rules and contract-owned target selection
items.rationale: The input contract evidence is explicit about resolution behavior, and the baseline distinguishes allowed provider-owned selection from disallowed inference in app glue.

items.body: For cross-service and composed flows, verification should prove service ownership remains visible, stop conditions remain reviewable, and consumer-side glue does not hide coordination. Cover the named handoffs between pipeline and enrichment, prioritization and outreach, and other declared coordination edges. For composed capabilities, verify child outcome propagation, lineage capture, and that the parent preserves clarification, denial, restriction, or approval behavior from owned child steps.
items.title: Verify visible service ownership across handoffs and composed workflows
items.rationale: The locked scenarios explicitly require visible coordination boundaries, and the composition evidence defines child-step propagation and lineage expectations.

items.body: Verification should cover multi-turn conversations where an initial safe result is followed by pressure to expand scope, bypass approval, or execute a draft. Expected behavior should preserve the original contract boundary across turns and link later stop conditions to the same conversation and actor context. Audit checks should require reconstructable records for actor identity, capability invoked, normalized parameters, governed outcome, task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id where applicable.
items.title: Verify multi-turn boundary preservation and reconstructable audit lineage
items.rationale: The baseline explicitly requires multi-turn governance continuity and reconstructable audit evidence, and the hard-mode multi-turn cases are central source coverage.

coverage.id: product_summary:governed_behavior_summary
coverage.label: Governed Behavior Summary
coverage.detail: The system returns governed bounded results for in-scope reads and drafts, asks for clarification instead of guessing missing critical inputs, restricts or masks results when actor scope is partial, denies raw export, hidden-internals, direct-send, and other out-of-scope or unsafe requests, and preserves those boundaries across multi-turn and multi-service flows.
coverage.section: Business Summary · Governed Behavior
coverage.mapping_note: This PM governed behavior summary is formalized explicitly in Product Intent Formalization on the Service Formalization page.
coverage.mapping_target_key: developer_definition.product_alignment.governed_behavior_formalization
coverage.mapping_target_label: Developer Design > Service Formalization > Product Intent Formalization > Governed Behavior Summary

coverage.id: product_summary:approval_posture_summary
coverage.label: Approval Posture Summary
coverage.detail: Operational preparation and execution are distinct. The system may generate bounded previews for follow-up tasks, reassignment plans, routing, or write-adjacent work when the actor is allowed, but it must stop at approval_required before any downstream mutation or send behavior, and approval state must remain reviewable.

coverage.mapping_note: This PM approval posture is formalized explicitly in Product Intent Formalization on the Service Formalization page.
coverage.mapping_target_key: developer_definition.product_alignment.approval_posture_formalization

identity.system_name: GTM Pipeline Q2 Review 0.4.5 Final RC 2

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not turn the showcase into a single unconstrained GTM super-agent or generic query-anything surface.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: Multiple sources state that the system must stay bounded through explicit capabilities and service boundaries rather than broad agent discretion.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not expose raw row-level CRM exports, raw SQL freedom, or arbitrary database exploration through the agent layer.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The sources repeatedly deny raw exports and position semantic, bounded reads as the intended product surface.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not allow direct-send outreach, silent routing, reassignment execution, follow-up execution, or any other downstream mutation without explicit approval.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The product is designed to separate preparation from execution and to stop at approval before side effects.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not use hidden prompt logic, benchmark-only repair code, or consumer-side instructions as the policy engine for authority, approval, denial, or recovery behavior.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The hard-mode governance source explicitly says execution policy must not move into prompts, recipes, or benchmark-only fixes.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not let users escalate actor authority or widen scope by claiming a different role in natural language.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: Actor-aware visibility, masking, and restriction are service-owned boundaries and must not be overridden conversationally.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not guess missing critical inputs such as quarter, target account, cohort, ranking basis, or routing target when the contract requires clarification.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The specs consistently require clarification instead of guessing for missing business-critical references.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not expose raw scoring features, model weights, hidden enrichment records, transcript dumps, or other unsupported internal evidence.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The intended explanation posture is bounded and reviewable, not a dump of backend internals or source corpora.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not collapse the system into one giant merged service or blur service ownership across pipeline, enrichment, prioritization, and outreach behaviors.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The full GTM showcase explicitly depends on separate bounded services with canonical owned capability surfaces.

business_goal_bindings.business_goal: Let revenue users inspect pipeline health, forecast posture, risk, bottlenecks, team performance, and product performance through bounded business capabilities.
business_goal_bindings.evidence_signal: Scenario and runtime evidence demonstrate: Let revenue users inspect pipeline health, forecast posture, risk, bottlenecks, team performance, and product performance through bounded business capabilities.
business_goal_bindings.verification_strategy: Verify that the selected services preserve the business goal: Let revenue users inspect pipeline health, forecast posture, risk, bottlenecks, team performance, and product performance through bounded business capabilities.
